plant_parts.name: leaf
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica leaf is rich in triterpenoid saponins, particularly asiaticoside, which has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaf also contains madecassoside and madecassic acid, which are believed to aid in wound healing and improve skin health. These bioactive compounds have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and skin disorders.

plant_parts.name: root
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica root is primarily used to treat anxiety and stress due to its adaptogenic properties, which help regulate the body's response to stress. It is also rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for skin health and wound healing. The root of this plant is also traditionally used to improve cognitive function and memory, as well as to support cardiovascular health.

plant_parts.name: whole plant
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica whole plant is traditionally used in various medicinal practices due to its rich content of bioactive compounds, including triterpenoids and alkaloids. The whole plant, particularly the leaves, stems, and roots, are used to treat a range of health conditions, from inflammation and wounds to anxiety and cognitive impairment. The medicinal properties of Centella asiatica whole plant are attributed to its ability to promote healing, improve cognitive function, and modulate the immune system.

plant_parts.name: rhizome
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica rhizome, also known as Gotu Kola, is a medicinal part used to promote skin health and wound healing. It is rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The rhizome is also used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and fever.

plant_parts.name: underground part
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica underground part, commonly known as the rhizome and roots, contain various bioactive compounds such as iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, contributing to the plant's medicinal value. The extracts from the underground parts of Centella asiatica are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and skin disorders.

plant_parts.name: stem
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica stem, which is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, has been traditionally used in various medicinal practices for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The stem is often used in herbal medicine to promote wound healing and improve skin health due to its high concentration of triterpenoid saponins. Additionally, the stem of Centella asiatica is also valued for its potential neuroprotective effects and its ability to enhance cognitive function.

plant_parts.name: aerial part
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica aerial part, also known as gotu kola, is rich in triterpenoids and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, stress, and skin issues, such as wound healing and eczema. The aerial part of Centella asiatica is also used to improve cognitive function and boost the immune system.

plant_parts.name: flower
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica flower has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and oxidative stress. The bioactive compounds present in the flower, such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madecassic acid, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, making the centella asiatica flower a valuable component in traditional medicine.

plant_parts.name: above-ground part
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica above-ground part, also known as gotu kola, is a traditional medicinal herb used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. The leaves, stems, and flowers of this plant are rich in triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and wound-healing effects, making Centella asiatica a popular natural remedy for various health conditions.

plant_parts.name: seed
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica seed contains triterpenoids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and are often used in traditional medicine for their therapeutic benefits. The seed is also a rich source of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Furthermore, the seed has been studied for its potential in treating various health conditions, including cardiovascular disease and neurodegenerative disorders.

plant_parts.name: tuber
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica tuber, also known as gotu kola tuber, is used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is believed to aid in wound healing and may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including arthritis and diabetes. Studies have also suggested that the tuber may possess neuroprotective effects, making it a subject of interest in the field of neuroscience.

plant_parts.name: corm
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica corm is a rich source of bioactive compounds, particularly triterpenoids and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The corm of Centella asiatica has been traditionally used in various folk medicine systems to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other inflammatory diseases. Extracts from the corm have been shown to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and improve skin health.

plant_parts.name: bark
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica bark is known to contain various bioactive compounds including triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The bark has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and cardiovascular diseases. It also exhibits antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential therapeutic benefits.

plant_parts.name: fruit
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica fruit, although not as widely researched as other parts of the plant, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to support overall health and well-being. Some studies suggest that the fruit may possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential. The fruit's active compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, may contribute to its potential health benefits.

plant_parts.name: petiole
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica petiole is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The petiole has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and inflammation. Its medicinal properties also make it a potential candidate for wound healing and tissue repair applications.

plant_parts.name: inflorescence
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica inflorescence contains a compound called asiaticoside, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The inflorescence of Centella asiatica is also rich in other bioactive compounds such as brahmoside and madecassoside, which contribute to its therapeutic potential. These compounds have been found to have neuroprotective and wound-healing effects, making Centella asiatica inflorescence a valuable component in traditional medicine.

plant_parts.name: vein
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica vein, also known as gotu kola, contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been found to promote wound healing and improve vein health by increasing collagen synthesis and improving blood vessel function. The saponins in Centella asiatica vein have also been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and improve circulation in the veins. Additionally, the flavonoids present in Centella asiatica vein may help to improve venous tone and reduce the risk of varicose veins.

plant_parts.name: stolon
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica stolon, also known as the creeping stems, is a rich source of triterpenoid saponins, particularly asiaticoside, which is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties. The stolon contains other bioactive compounds such as brahmoside and centellase, which may contribute to its medicinal benefits. Extracts from the stolon have been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including skin ulcers, eczema, and traumatic injuries.

plant_parts.name: petal
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica petal, also known as the extract of the Asian pennywort flower, contains flavonoids and phenolic acids which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The petal is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions such as eczema and acne due to its soothing and calming effects. Additionally, the petal extract has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable component in wound care and skin treatments.

plant_parts.name: stamen
plant_parts.desc: Centella asiatica stamen, a lesser-known medicinal part of the plant, contains saponins and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds may help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making the stamen a potential treatment for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Additionally, the stamen's bioactive compounds may also contribute to its potential benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving cognitive function.
